docs.title: VisionSpec
docs.text: Multi-domain specification orchestration for humans and AI agents.

docs.title: What is VisionSpec?
docs.text:
VisionSpec bridges the gap between organizational intent (MRD, PRD, UXD) and executable specifications for AI coding agents. It provides a structured workflow for:

  • Dual-methodology system - Separate requirements (WHAT) from implementation (HOW) methodologies
  • Methodology profiles - AWS, Google, Stripe, Lean Startup, Design Thinking, JTBD
  • AIDLC workflow - Full AWS AI-DLC integration with 12 document types across 3 phases
  • V2MOM strategic planning - Company, department, and team-level strategic alignment
  • Domain-specific authoring - Separate specs for PM, UX, Engineering
  • GTM synthesis - LLM-generated press releases, FAQs, narratives (Working Backwards)
  • Technical synthesis - LLM-generated TRD, TPD, IRD from source specs
  • Structured evaluation - Per-domain LLM judges with 1-5 numeric scoring
  • Reconciliation - Conflict detection and tradeoff resolution
  • Target adapters - Export to SpecKit, GSD, GasTown, GasCity, AWS AI-DLC, OpenSpec

docs.title: Working Backwards Flow
docs.text:
VisionSpec implements Amazon's Working Backwards methodology:

1. MARKET PROBLEM (human-authored)
   mrd.md
       ↓
2. WORKING BACKWARDS (synthesized, editable)
   press.md  →  faq.md  →  prd.md
   (vision)     (scope)    (requirements)
       ↓
3. STAKEHOLDER REVIEW (synthesized, editable)
   narrative-1p.md / narrative-6p.md
       ↓
4. USER EXPERIENCE (human-authored)
   uxd.md
       ↓
5. TECHNICAL SPECS (synthesized, editable)
   trd.md  →  tpd.md  →  ird.md
   (design)   (tests)    (infra)
       ↓
6. RECONCILIATION
   All approved specs → spec.md
       ↓
7. AI EXECUTION
   spec.md → SpecKit | GSD | GasTown | GasCity
       ↓
8. POST-SHIP ALIGNMENT
   spec.md + reality → current-truth.md

All synthesized documents are committed to git and can be reviewed, edited, and refined by humans or collaboratively with AI assistants.

See the Working Backwards Guide for the full methodology.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install
go install github.com/ProductBuildersHQ/visionspec/cmd/visionspec@v0.13.0

# Initialize a new project
visionspec init user-onboarding

# Initialize with a methodology profile
visionspec init my-product --profile aws

# Initialize with a stage profile
visionspec init my-feature --profile startup

# Validate project structure
visionspec lint

# Check project status
visionspec status

docs.title: Readiness Gates
docs.text:
VisionSpec tracks project readiness through configurable gates:

Gate Description Required specs present All required source specs exist Evaluations passing No blocking evaluation findings Approvals obtained All required specs have approvals Execution spec generated spec.md has been created

docs.title: Multiple Output Formats
docs.text:
Status reports can be generated in multiple formats for different use cases:

  • Text - Terminal output with icons
  • JSON - Programmatic access
  • HTML - Browser-viewable reports with traffic light indicators
  • Markdown - For embedding in documentation

docs.title: MCP Integration
docs.text: VisionSpec includes an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for integration with AI coding assistants like Claude Code and Kiro CLI.

docs.title: Configuration Profiles
docs.text: VisionSpec includes two types of profiles:

docs.title: Stage-Based Profiles
docs.text: Profile Required Specs Use Case 0-1 hypothesis Idea validation phase startup prd Pre-PMF startups growth prd, uxd, faq 1-N scaling phase enterprise mrd, prd, uxd, trd, press, faq, spec Post-PMF enterprises

docs.title: Methodology Profiles
docs.text:
Profile Methodology Best For aws Working Backwards Customer-centric products google Design Docs + RFC Engineering-heavy orgs stripe API-First Platform/API products lean-startup Build-Measure-Learn Early validation design-thinking Stanford d.school Human-centered design jtbd Jobs to be Done Customer motivations

See Frameworks for detailed methodology documentation.

# List available profiles
visionspec profiles list

# Initialize with a methodology profile
visionspec init my-product --profile aws

# Export a profile for customization
visionspec profiles export enterprise ./my-profile

# Use a custom profile
visionspec init my-project --profile-dir ./my-profile

Organizations can create custom profiles with their own templates and rubrics. See the Custom Profiles Guide for details.

docs.title: Project Status
docs.text:
See the ROADMAP for detailed implementation status and Release Notes for the latest release.

Current Version: v0.13.0

Component Status CLI (init, lint, status, eval, synthesize, reconcile) Complete MCP Server (draft workflow, eval) Complete Evaluation Engine (v2 schema with 1-5 scoring) Complete GTM & Technical Synthesis (Press, FAQ, PRD, TRD, TPD, IRD) Complete Reconciliation Complete Export (SpecKit, GSD, GasTown, GasCity, AIDLC) Complete Graphize Integration Complete Profiles & Composability Complete Methodology Profiles (AWS, Google, Stripe, Lean, DT, JTBD) Complete AI Workflow Orchestration (rules) Complete AIDLC Workflow Package (pkg/aidlc/) Complete Dual-Methodology System Complete V2MOM Strategic Planning Profiles Complete

docs.title: Context Sources
docs.text: Context sources enable visionspec to gather information from your existing codebases, requirement graphs, and external tools to ground spec synthesis in reality.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
When synthesizing technical specs (TRD, IRD), visionspec can automatically gather context from:

  • Git Repositories - Code structure, dependencies, API schemas
  • Graphize Graphs - Requirement traceability data
  • MCP Servers - External tools like Jira, Confluence, Google Docs
  • Local Files - Architecture docs, design decisions

This context is included in LLM prompts to ensure generated specs reflect your actual codebase and organizational knowledge.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Add context sources to your visionspec.yaml:

name: my-feature
context:
  repositories:
    - path: /path/to/backend
      graphize: auto
    - url: https://github.com/org/frontend.git
      branch: main
      sparse:
        - src/components
        - src/api

  graphize:
    - path: /path/to/requirements/.graphize
      name: product-requirements

  files:
    - path: docs/architecture.md
      type: architecture
    - path: docs/adr/
      type: decisions

  mcp_servers:
    jira:
      command: npx
      args: ["-y", "@anthropic/jira-mcp"]
      config:
        project: PROJ
        jql: "status != Done"

    confluence:
      command: npx
      args: ["-y", "@anthropic/confluence-mcp"]
      config:
        space: ENGINEERING

  cache_ttl: 5m

docs.title: Local Repository
docs.text:
context:
  repositories:
    - path: /absolute/path/to/repo
      analyze:
        - structure      # Directory tree
        - dependencies   # go.mod, package.json, etc.
        - api_schemas    # OpenAPI, GraphQL, Proto
        - readme         # README content
      max_depth: 5       # Directory tree depth
      include:
        - "**/*.go"
        - "**/*.ts"
      exclude:
        - "**/vendor/**"
        - "**/node_modules/**"

docs.title: Remote Repository
docs.text:
context:
  repositories:
    - url: https://github.com/org/repo.git
      branch: main
      sparse:
        - src/
        - pkg/

Remote repositories are cloned to a cache directory (/tmp/visionspec-repos/). Shallow clones are used for performance.

docs.title: Extracted Information
docs.text: Analysis Data Extracted structure Directory tree, file sizes dependencies Package names, versions (go.mod, package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml) api_schemas OpenAPI/Swagger routes, GraphQL queries/mutations, Proto services readme README content (truncated to 5KB)

docs.title: Graphize Integration
docs.text: Graphize provides requirement traceability graphs.

docs.title: Auto-Detection
docs.text:
When graphize: auto is set on a repository, visionspec automatically loads graphs from .graphize/ directories.

context:
  repositories:
    - path: /path/to/repo
      graphize: auto  # Detect .graphize/ in repo

docs.title: Standalone Graph
docs.text:
context:
  graphize:
    - path: /path/to/project
      name: requirements
      include_nodes:
        - requirement
        - decision
        - constraint
      include_edges:
        - traces_to
        - depends_on

docs.title: Extracted Information
docs.text: Data Description Nodes Requirements, decisions, constraints, user stories Edges Traceability links between nodes Coverage Percentage of requirements linked to code/tests

docs.title: MCP Servers
docs.text: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers provide access to external tools.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
context:
  mcp_servers:
    server-name:
      command: npx       # Command to start server
      args: ["-y", "@anthropic/jira-mcp"]
      env:               # Environment variables
        JIRA_URL: https://company.atlassian.net
        JIRA_TOKEN: ${JIRA_TOKEN}
      config:            # Server-specific config
        project: PROJ
        jql: "status != Done ORDER BY updated DESC"
      timeout: 30s

docs.title: Supported Servers
docs.text: Server Package Data Jira @anthropic/jira-mcp Issues, epics, stories Confluence @anthropic/confluence-mcp Pages, spaces Google Docs @anthropic/gdocs-mcp Documents Linear @anthropic/linear-mcp Issues, projects Notion @anthropic/notion-mcp Pages, databases

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Use ${VAR} syntax to reference environment variables:

mcp_servers:
  jira:
    env:
      JIRA_TOKEN: ${JIRA_API_TOKEN}

docs.title: Local Files
docs.text:
Include individual files as context:

context:
  files:
    - path: docs/architecture.md
      type: architecture

    - path: docs/api-spec.yaml
      type: api_spec

    - path: DECISIONS.md
      type: decisions

    - path: diagrams/system.puml
      type: diagram

docs.title: File Types
docs.text: Type Description architecture System architecture documentation api_spec API specifications (OpenAPI, GraphQL) readme README files decisions ADRs, decision logs diagram PlantUML, Mermaid diagrams requirements Requirements documents config Configuration files document Generic documents

docs.title: Gather Context
docs.text:
# Gather from all configured sources
visionspec context gather

# Gather with JSON output
visionspec context gather --format json

# Force refresh (ignore cache)
visionspec context gather --refresh

docs.title: Show Context
docs.text:
# Display context summary
visionspec context show

docs.title: Save Snapshot
docs.text:
# Save context to file for CI/reproducibility
visionspec context save --output context-snapshot.json

docs.title: List Sources
docs.text:
# Show configured sources
visionspec context sources

docs.title: Context in Synthesis
docs.text:
Context is automatically used when synthesizing TRD and IRD:

# TRD synthesis includes context
visionspec synthesize trd

# Skip context gathering
visionspec synthesize trd --no-context

The context summary is included in the LLM prompt to ground technical decisions.

docs.title: Caching
docs.text:
Context is cached in memory with configurable TTL:

context:
  cache_ttl: 5m  # Default: 5 minutes

Use --refresh to bypass cache:

visionspec context gather --refresh

docs.title: Snapshots
docs.text:
Save context snapshots for reproducibility:

# Save snapshot
visionspec context save -o context.json

# Use snapshot in CI
visionspec synthesize trd --context-file context.json

Snapshots contain all gathered context and can be version controlled.

docs.title: Writing Custom Sources
docs.text:
Implement the Source interface:

package custom

import (
    "context"
    ctx "github.com/ProductBuildersHQ/visionspec/pkg/context"
)

type MySource struct {
    // ...
}

func (s *MySource) Name() string {
    return "custom:my-source"
}

func (s *MySource) Type() ctx.SourceType {
    return ctx.SourceTypeFile // or custom type
}

func (s *MySource) Fetch(c context.Context) (*ctx.ContextData, error) {
    // Fetch and return context data
    return &ctx.ContextData{
        Source: s.Name(),
        Type:   s.Type(),
        // ...
    }, nil
}

func (s *MySource) String() string {
    return "My Custom Source"
}

Register with the aggregator:

agg := ctx.NewAggregator("project", cfg)
agg.AddSource(&MySource{})

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use sparse checkout for large repositories to reduce clone time
  2. Set appropriate TTL - longer for stable codebases, shorter for active development
  3. Use snapshots in CI for reproducible builds
  4. Filter nodes/edges in graphize to reduce context size
  5. Truncate large files - context is automatically truncated for LLM token limits

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: VisionSpec provides a command-line interface for managing specifications.

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text: Flag Short Description --project -p Project name or path --verbose -v Enable verbose output --help -h Show help --version Show version

docs.title: Project Setup
docs.text: Command Description init Initialize a new project create Create specs from templates lint Validate directory structure status Show project status profiles Manage configuration profiles

docs.title: Spec Workflow
docs.text: Command Description eval Evaluate specs using LLM judges render Render evaluation files to markdown synthesize Generate GTM/technical specs from sources reconcile Generate unified execution spec approve Approve a spec for reconciliation watch Watch spec files and auto-run eval version Manage spec version history

docs.title: Export & Integration
docs.text: Command Description export Export to target execution system targets List available export targets serve Start MCP server docs Generate MkDocs documentation rules Manage workflow rules for AI assistants

docs.title: Execution Integration
docs.text: Command Description align Check spec-to-reality alignment drift Detect spec-to-code drift generate Generate test stubs from TPD sync Sync status with execution targets metrics View evaluation metrics dashboard hooks Manage Git hooks (install, uninstall, status)

docs.title: Context & Traceability
docs.text: Command Description context Gather codebase context graph Manage requirement graphs

docs.title: Usage Examples
docs.text:
# Initialize a project
visionspec init user-onboarding

# Lint all projects
visionspec lint

# Lint specific project
visionspec lint user-onboarding

# Check status
visionspec status -p user-onboarding

# JSON output
visionspec status -p user-onboarding --format json

# Generate HTML report
visionspec status -p user-onboarding --format html > status.html

# CI mode (exit non-zero if not ready)
visionspec status -p user-onboarding --ci

# List export targets
visionspec targets

docs.title: align
docs.text: Check alignment between specifications and current implementation truth.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: visionspec align [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The align command verifies that specifications match the current state of the implementation. Unlike drift which detects divergence, align performs a structured comparison against a documented "current truth" to identify discrepancies that need resolution.

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Description -p, --project Project name --with-context Include codebase context in analysis --context-file Path to pre-gathered context file --format Output format: text, json, markdown (default: text) --ci CI mode: exit non-zero if misaligned

docs.title: Basic Alignment Check
docs.text:
# Check alignment for project
visionspec align -p myproject

docs.title: With Codebase Context
docs.text:
# Include live codebase context
visionspec align -p myproject --with-context

# Use pre-gathered context file
visionspec align -p myproject --context-file context.json

docs.title: Output Formats
docs.text:
# Text output (default)
visionspec align -p myproject

# JSON output for programmatic use
visionspec align -p myproject --format json

# Markdown output for documentation
visionspec align -p myproject --format markdown > alignment-report.md

docs.title: CI Integration
docs.text:
# Exit non-zero if misaligned (for CI pipelines)
visionspec align -p myproject --ci

docs.title: Missing Feature
docs.text:
Specified in requirements but not present:

ALIGN-001 [high] missing_feature
  Description: OAuth2 login not implemented
  Spec Ref: REQ-AUTH-003
  Suggestion: Implement OAuth2 authentication flow

docs.title: Undocumented Code
docs.text:
Implementation exists but not specified:

ALIGN-002 [medium] undocumented_code
  Description: Session timeout logic exists but not specified
  Code Ref: pkg/auth/session.go:120
  Suggestion: Add session management requirements to TRD

docs.title: Diverged
docs.text:
Both exist but behavior differs:

ALIGN-003 [high] diverged
  Description: Password requirements differ from spec
  Spec Ref: REQ-AUTH-001
  Code Ref: pkg/auth/validation.go:45
  Suggestion: Align password validation rules

docs.title: Alignment Report
docs.text:
Example JSON output:

{
  "project": "user-onboarding",
  "generated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "discrepancies": [
    {
      "type": "missing_feature",
      "spec_ref": "REQ-AUTH-003",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "OAuth2 login not implemented"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total": 3,
    "aligned": 45,
    "discrepancies": 3,
    "alignment_percentage": 93.75
  }
}

docs.title: Current Truth
docs.text:
The alignment check compares against a current-truth.md file that documents the actual implementation state:

# Current Truth

## Implemented Features
- [x] Basic authentication
- [x] Password reset
- [ ] OAuth2 login (in progress)

## API Endpoints
- POST /api/auth/login - implemented
- POST /api/auth/logout - implemented
- POST /api/auth/oauth - not implemented

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• drift - Detect spec-to-code drift
  • context - Gather codebase context
  • status - Check project status

docs.title: approve
docs.text: Mark a specification as approved for reconciliation.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: visionspec approve <spec-type> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The approve command marks a specification as approved and ready for reconciliation. Approvals are required before running visionspec reconcile to generate the unified execution spec.

Approvals are recorded in visionspec.yaml with:

  • Approver identifier
  • Timestamp
  • Optional comment

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description spec-type Type of spec to approve (mrd, prd, uxd, trd, ird, etc.)

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --approver string $USER Approver email or identifier --comment string "" Approval comment

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Approve PRD with default approver
visionspec approve prd

# Approve TRD with specific approver
visionspec approve trd --approver=eng@company.com

# Approve with comment
visionspec approve mrd --approver=pm@company.com --comment="Reviewed in spec review meeting"

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
✓ Approved prd by john
  Comment: Reviewed in spec review meeting

docs.title: Approval Workflow
docs.text:
1. Write spec - Create or synthesize the specification
  2. Evaluate - Run visionspec eval <type> to check quality
  3. Review - Human review of spec content
  4. Approve - Mark as approved with this command
  5. Reconcile - Run visionspec reconcile after all approvals

docs.title: Required Approvals
docs.text:
The specs required for reconciliation depend on your profile:

Profile Required Approvals startup prd growth prd, uxd enterprise mrd, prd, uxd, trd

docs.title: Checking Approval Status
docs.text:
Use visionspec status to see which specs are approved:

visionspec status

# Approvals:
#   ✓ mrd - approved by pm@co on 2024-01-10
#   ✓ prd - approved by pm@co on 2024-01-11
#   ✗ uxd - not approved
#   ✗ trd - not approved

docs.title: Storage
docs.text:
Approvals are stored in visionspec.yaml:

approvals:
  mrd:
    approver: pm@company.com
    approved_at: 2024-01-10T14:30:00Z
    comment: "Reviewed in planning meeting"
  prd:
    approver: pm@company.com
    approved_at: 2024-01-11T09:00:00Z

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• eval - Evaluate specs before approval
  • reconcile - Reconcile after all approvals
  • status - Check approval status

docs.title: context
docs.text: Gather and manage codebase context for grounding spec synthesis.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: visionspec context <subcommand> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The context command manages codebase context that grounds technical spec synthesis in reality. Context is gathered from git repositories, graphize requirement graphs, local files, and MCP servers.

docs.title: Subcommands
docs.text: Subcommand Description gather Collect context from all configured sources show Display current context summary save Save context snapshot to file sources List configured context sources

docs.title: context gather
docs.text:
Gather context from all configured sources.

visionspec context gather [flags]

Flags:

Flag Type Default Description --timeout duration 2m Timeout for gathering --format string text Output format: text, json --refresh bool false Refresh cache before gathering

Example:

# Gather context
visionspec context gather

# Output as JSON
visionspec context gather --format json

# Force refresh cached data
visionspec context gather --refresh

# Custom timeout
visionspec context gather --timeout 5m

docs.title: context show
docs.text:
Display the current context summary from the last gather or saved snapshot.

visionspec context show

If no snapshot exists, prompts to run gather first.

docs.title: context save
docs.text:
Save gathered context to a snapshot file for CI reproducibility.

visionspec context save [flags]

Flags:

Flag Type Default Description -o, --output string .context-snapshot.json Output file path

Example:

# Save to default location
visionspec context save

# Save to custom path
visionspec context save -o artifacts/context.json

docs.title: context sources
docs.text:
List all configured context sources.

visionspec context sources

Output:

Configured context sources:

Git Repositories:
  - /path/to/repo
    (graphize: auto)

Graphize Graphs:
  - spec-graph (/path/to/.graphize)

Local Files:
  - architecture.md (architecture)
  - docs/adr/*.md (adr)

MCP Servers:
  - jira (not yet implemented)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configure context sources in visionspec.yaml:

context:
  # Git repositories to analyze
  repositories:
    - path: "."
      include_structure: true
      include_deps: true
      include_apis: true
      graphize: auto
    - url: "https://github.com/org/repo"
      branch: main
      shallow: true

  # Graphize requirement graphs
  graphize:
    - path: ".graphize"
      name: "spec-graph"
      include_nodes: true
      include_edges: true

  # Local documentation files
  files:
    - path: "docs/architecture.md"
      type: architecture
    - path: "docs/adr/*.md"
      type: adr
    - path: "api/openapi.yaml"
      type: api_spec

  # MCP servers for external tools
  mcp_servers:
    jira:
      command: "npx"
      args: ["-y", "@anthropic/mcp-jira"]
      tools: ["get_issue", "search_issues"]

  # Cache settings
  cache_ttl: 30m

docs.title: Repository Analysis
docs.text:
When analyzing git repositories, the context includes:

  • Structure - Directory tree and file organization
  • Dependencies - Package dependencies (go.mod, package.json, etc.)
  • APIs - OpenAPI specs, GraphQL schemas, protobuf definitions
  • README - Repository documentation

docs.title: Auto-Detection
docs.text: If no repositories are configured, visionspec auto-detects the repository at the project root.

docs.title: Snapshots
docs.text:
Context snapshots enable reproducible synthesis in CI:

# In CI pipeline
visionspec context save -o context-snapshot.json

# Later, use saved context
visionspec synthesize trd --context-file context-snapshot.json

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Context Sources Guide - Comprehensive configuration guide
  • synthesize - Uses context for TRD/IRD synthesis

docs.title: create
docs.text: Create a new spec file from a template.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: visionspec create <spec-type> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The create command scaffolds a new specification file from an embedded template. This provides a starting point for writing specs with the correct structure and sections.

The command must be run from within a visionspec project directory (where visionspec.yaml exists).

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description spec-type Type of spec to create

docs.title: Supported Spec Types
docs.text: Type Category Description mrd Source Market Requirements Document prd Source Product Requirements Document uxd Source User Experience Design press GTM Press Release (Working Backwards) faq GTM FAQ Document narrative-1p GTM 1-Page Narrative narrative-6p GTM 6-Page Narrative trd Technical Technical Requirements Document ird Technical Infrastructure Requirements Document

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --force bool false Overwrite existing file

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Create a new MRD
visionspec create mrd

# Create a PRD
visionspec create prd

# Create a Press Release (Working Backwards)
visionspec create press

# Overwrite existing TRD
visionspec create trd --force

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
The command creates a file in the appropriate directory:

  • Source specs: source/{type}.md
  • GTM specs: gtm/{type}.md
  • Technical specs: technical/{type}.md

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• init - Initialize a new project with --with-templates
  • synthesize - Generate specs from source documents

docs.title: docs
docs.text: Generate MkDocs-compatible documentation files.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: visionspec docs <subcommand> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The docs command generates markdown files for MkDocs integration. It creates index pages for projects and the main specs landing page.

docs.title: Subcommands
docs.text: Subcommand Description generate Generate all index.md files project Generate index.md for a specific project

docs.title: docs generate
docs.text:
Generate index.md files for all projects and the specs landing page.

visionspec docs generate [flags]

Flags:

Flag Type Default Description --with-graph bool false Include traceability metrics from spec graph

Output files:

  • docs/specs/index.md - Specs landing page with project listing
  • docs/specs/{project}/index.md - Project index with status and specs table

Example:

# Generate all documentation
visionspec docs generate

# Include graph metrics
visionspec docs generate --with-graph

docs.title: docs project
docs.text:
Generate index.md for a specific project.

visionspec docs project <project-name> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Type Default Description --with-graph bool false Include traceability metrics from spec graph

Example:

# Generate index for specific project
visionspec docs project user-onboarding

# With graph metrics
visionspec docs project user-onboarding --with-graph

docs.title: Project Index ({project}/index.md)
docs.text:
• Status badge (ready/not ready)
  • Project description
  • Specs table with links
  • Traceability metrics (if --with-graph)

docs.title: Specs Landing (specs/index.md)
docs.text:
• Projects table with status and progress
  • Links to CONSTITUTION.md and ROADMAP.md
  • Cross-project metrics summary

docs.title: Graph Metrics
docs.text:
When using --with-graph, includes:

  • Total requirements count
  • User stories count
  • Trace coverage percentage
  • Conflict count

Requires running visionspec graph extract first.

docs.title: MkDocs Integration
docs.text:
The generated files integrate with your MkDocs site. Add to mkdocs.yml:

nav:
  - Specifications:
      - Overview: specs/index.md
      - user-onboarding: specs/user-onboarding/index.md
      - payment-flow: specs/payment-flow/index.md

docs.title: Example Output
docs.text:
specs/index.md:

docs.title: drift
docs.text: Detect divergence between specifications and implementation.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: visionspec drift [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The drift command compares specifications against the actual codebase to identify divergence. It detects:

  • Unimplemented: Requirements in spec but not in code
  • Undocumented: Features in code but not in spec
  • Mismatch: Both exist but differ

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Description -p, --project Project name --severity Filter by severity: critical, high, medium, low --format Output format: text, json, markdown (default: text) --ci CI mode: exit non-zero if drift detected

docs.title: Basic Drift Detection
docs.text:
# Detect drift in project
visionspec drift -p myproject

docs.title: Filter by Severity
docs.text:
# Show only critical and high severity drift
visionspec drift -p myproject --severity high

# Show only critical drift
visionspec drift -p myproject --severity critical

docs.title: Output Formats
docs.text:
# Text output (default)
visionspec drift -p myproject

# JSON output for programmatic use
visionspec drift -p myproject --format json

# Markdown output for documentation
visionspec drift -p myproject --format markdown > drift-report.md

docs.title: CI Integration
docs.text:
# Exit non-zero if drift detected (for CI pipelines)
visionspec drift -p myproject --ci

docs.title: Unimplemented
docs.text:
Requirements specified but not found in code:

DRIFT-001 [high] unimplemented
  Description: User authentication endpoint not implemented
  Spec Ref: REQ-AUTH-001
  Suggestion: Implement POST /api/auth/login endpoint

docs.title: Undocumented
docs.text:
Code features not covered by specifications:

DRIFT-002 [medium] undocumented
  Description: Rate limiting middleware exists but not specified
  Code Ref: pkg/middleware/ratelimit.go:45
  Suggestion: Add rate limiting requirements to TRD

docs.title: Mismatch
docs.text:
Both exist but differ in behavior:

DRIFT-003 [high] mismatch
  Description: API returns 401 but spec says 403
  Spec Ref: API-AUTH-002
  Code Ref: pkg/handlers/auth.go:78
  Suggestion: Align error codes between spec and implementation

docs.title: Severity Levels
docs.text: Severity Description critical Blocking issues that must be resolved high Important divergence affecting functionality medium Notable differences that should be addressed low Minor inconsistencies

docs.title: Drift Report
docs.text:
Example JSON output:

{
  "project": "user-onboarding",
  "generated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "items": [
    {
      "id": "DRIFT-001",
      "type": "unimplemented",
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "api",
      "description": "Missing /users/{id} DELETE endpoint",
      "spec_ref": "REQ-USER-005",
      "suggestion": "Implement user deletion endpoint"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total": 5,
    "by_type": {
      "unimplemented": 2,
      "undocumented": 2,
      "mismatch": 1
    },
    "by_severity": {
      "critical": 0,
      "high": 2,
      "medium": 2,
      "low": 1
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Context Integration
docs.text:
Drift detection uses the context infrastructure to analyze the codebase:

# Gather context first for better analysis
visionspec context gather -p myproject

# Then run drift detection
visionspec drift -p myproject

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• context - Gather codebase context
  • sync - Synchronize execution state
  • status - Check project status

docs.title: eval
docs.text: Evaluate specifications using LLM-as-a-Judge.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: visionspec eval [spec-type] [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The eval command evaluates specification documents against rubrics using an LLM judge. It checks for quality, completeness, and adherence to best practices.

Evaluation results are saved to eval/{spec-type}.json and include:

  • Overall score (1-5 integer scale)
  • Pass/fail decision with blocking reason codes
  • Per-dimension scores with confidence values
  • Findings with reason codes for automated repair

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description spec-type Specific spec type to evaluate (optional if using category flags)

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --all bool false Evaluate all specs --source bool false Evaluate source specs only (mrd, prd, uxd) --gtm bool false Evaluate GTM docs only (press, faq, narrative) --technical bool false Evaluate technical docs only (trd, ird)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Evaluate a specific spec
visionspec eval prd

# Evaluate all specs
visionspec eval --all

# Evaluate only source specs
visionspec eval --source

# Evaluate GTM documents
visionspec eval --gtm

# Evaluate technical specs
visionspec eval --technical

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
The command prints evaluation status for each spec:

⋯ Evaluating prd...
✓ prd: 4/5 (Good) PASS [conf: 88%]

⋯ Evaluating trd...
✗ trd: 2/5 (Major Revisions) FAIL [conf: 75%]
  Blocking: SEC-NO_AUTH, ARCH-NO_API

docs.title: Score Scale (1-5)
docs.text: Score Label Description 5 Excellent Exceeds expectations 4 Good Meets expectations well 3 Acceptable Meets minimum requirements 2 Major Revisions Significant work needed 1 Unacceptable Does not meet requirements

docs.title: Evaluation Results (v2)
docs.text:
Results are saved to eval/{spec-type}.json in v2 format:

{
  "schemaVersion": "v2",
  "scoreV2": 4,
  "decision": "conditional",
  "pass": false,
  "confidence": 0.78,
  "blocking": ["REQ-NO_CRITERIA", "METRIC-UNMEASURABLE"],
  "dimensions": [
    {
      "id": "requirements",
      "name": "Requirements Clarity",
      "score": 3,
      "severity": "minor",
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "reasonCodes": ["REQ-NO_CRITERIA"],
      "findings": [
        {
          "category": "requirements",
          "severity": "medium",
          "code": "REQ-NO_CRITERIA",
          "message": "FR-2.3 lacks acceptance criteria",
          "location": "FR-2.3"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "findings": [...]
}

docs.title: Key Fields
docs.text: Field Description schemaVersion Format version ("v2") scoreV2 Overall score (1-5 integer) pass Boolean pass/fail gate confidence Evaluation confidence (0.0-1.0) blocking Reason codes that caused failure dimensions Per-dimension breakdown with scores

docs.title: Reason Codes
docs.text:
Findings include standardized reason codes that enable automated repair:

Prefix Domain Example REQ- Requirements REQ-AMBIGUOUS, REQ-NO_CRITERIA SEC- Security SEC-NO_AUTH, SEC-PRIVACY UX- UX/Accessibility UX-NO_ARIA, UX-NO_ERROR_STATE ARCH- Architecture ARCH-NO_API, ARCH-GAP

See Reason Codes Reference for the complete list.

docs.title: Severity Levels
docs.text: Severity Description Impact critical Spec cannot be used Automatic fail, added to blocking high Major issues Likely fail, added to blocking medium Should be addressed May affect score low Minor improvements Informational info Observations No impact on score

docs.title: Pass Criteria
docs.text:
A spec passes evaluation when:

  • Score >= 3 (Acceptable)
  • No critical findings
  • No high-severity findings (or within threshold)
  • No blocking reason codes

docs.title: Confidence Values
docs.text:
The evaluation includes confidence scores (0.0-1.0):

Confidence Interpretation 0.9+ Very confident in assessment 0.7-0.9 Confident 0.5-0.7 Somewhat confident, consider review < 0.5 Low confidence, needs human review

Low confidence evaluations are flagged with "Needs Review" in VisionStudio.

docs.title: LLM Configuration
docs.text:
The LLM used for evaluation is configured in visionspec.yaml:

llm:
  provider: anthropic
  model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  temperature: 0.3

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Reason Codes Reference - Complete reason code documentation
  • synthesize - Generate specs with optional evaluation
  • approve - Approve specs that pass evaluation

docs.title: export
docs.text: Export the reconciled spec to target execution systems.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: visionspec export <target> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The export command transforms the unified spec.md into formats compatible with AI coding agent execution systems. Each target produces different output files optimized for specific workflows.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before exporting, you must have a reconciled spec.md:

visionspec reconcile

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description target Target system: speckit, gsd, gastown, gascity, aidlc

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --dry-run bool false Show what would be exported without writing --output string "" Output directory (default: export/<target>)

docs.title: speckit
docs.text:
GitHub Spec-Kit format for spec-driven development.

Output files:

  • spec.md - Unified specification
  • plan.md - Implementation plan with phases
  • tasks.md - Task breakdown

Features:

  • Sequential task execution
  • Constitution sync to .specify/memory/
  • Branch numbering support

docs.title: gsd
docs.text:
Get Shit Done methodology format.

Output files:

  • PLAN.md - Plan with YAML frontmatter (must_haves, truths, artifacts)
  • STATE.md - Progress tracking
  • config.json - GSD configuration

Features:

  • Wave-based parallel execution
  • Artifact verification
  • Progress state tracking

docs.title: gastown
docs.text:
GasTown multi-agent formula format.

Output files:

  • formula.toml - Formula definition (convoy/workflow/expansion)
  • beads/*.toml - Individual task beads

Features:

  • DAG-based execution
  • Bead dependencies
  • Priority ordering

docs.title: gascity
docs.text:
GasCity multi-agent orchestration format.

Output files:

  • city.toml - City configuration with agents and orders

Features:

  • Agent role definitions
  • Order (task) dependencies
  • Orchestration modes (orchestrated, autonomous, hybrid)

docs.title: aidlc
docs.text:
AWS AI-DLC Workflows format for the AI Development Lifecycle.

Output files:

  • vision-document.md - Vision and goals (from MRD/Press/PRD)
  • technical-environment.md - Technical landscape (from TRD/IRD/context)
  • imported-requirements.md - Combined requirements from spec.md

Features:

  • Three-phase lifecycle (Inception, Construction, Operations)
  • Approval gates for requirements refinement
  • Multi-agent specialist phases
  • Integration with existing VisionSpec specs

Usage with AIDLC:

After export, use the trigger pattern: "Using AI-DLC, develop this feature based on the vision document."

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Export to SpecKit
visionspec export speckit

# Export to GSD with custom output directory
visionspec export gsd --output ./my-gsd-export

# Dry run to see what would be created
visionspec export gastown --dry-run

# Export to GasCity
visionspec export gascity

# Export to AWS AI-DLC Workflows
visionspec export aidlc

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
⋯ Exporting to speckit...
✓ Exported to SpecKit format
  Output: docs/specs/my-project/export/speckit
  Files:
    - spec.md
    - plan.md
    - tasks.md

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Target-specific configuration in visionspec.yaml:

targets:
  default: speckit
  speckit:
    enabled: true
    output_dir: export/speckit
    branch_numbering: sequential
  gsd:
    model_profile: balanced
  gastown:
    formula_type: convoy
    rig: my-rig
  gascity:
    city_dir: export/gascity
  aidlc:
    enabled: true
    output_dir: .aidlc

docs.title: Constitution Sync
docs.text: For SpecKit exports, if CONSTITUTION.md exists, it's synced to .specify/memory/constitution.md for agent memory integration.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• reconcile - Generate spec.md before export
  • targets - List available targets

docs.title: generate
docs.text: Generate artifacts from specifications.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: visionspec generate <subcommand> [flags]

docs.title: tests
docs.text:
Generate test stubs from Test Plan Document (TPD).

visionspec generate tests [flags]

Flags:

Flag Description -p, --project Project name --lang Target language: go, ts, py (default: go) --framework Test framework: testing, testify, jest, vitest, pytest --output Output directory (default: ./generated-tests) --group-by Grouping strategy: type, file, priority (default: type)

docs.title: Generate Go Tests
docs.text:
# Generate Go test stubs using standard testing package
visionspec generate tests -p myproject --lang go

# Generate with testify framework
visionspec generate tests -p myproject --lang go --framework testify

# Specify output directory
visionspec generate tests -p myproject --lang go --output ./tests/generated

docs.title: Generate TypeScript Tests
docs.text:
# Generate Jest test stubs
visionspec generate tests -p myproject --lang ts --framework jest

# Generate Vitest test stubs
visionspec generate tests -p myproject --lang ts --framework vitest

docs.title: Generate Python Tests
docs.text:
# Generate pytest test stubs
visionspec generate tests -p myproject --lang py

docs.title: TPD Sections Parsed
docs.text:
The generator extracts test cases from these TPD sections:

Section Test Type Section 3: Functional Tests Unit and functional tests Section 4.1: API Tests API endpoint tests Section 5.1: Journey Tests E2E and integration tests

docs.title: Go Output
docs.text:
generated-tests/
├── functional_test.go     # Functional test stubs
├── api_test.go            # API test stubs
└── journey_test.go        # Journey/E2E test stubs

docs.title: TypeScript Output
docs.text:
generated-tests/
├── functional.test.ts     # Functional test stubs
├── api.test.ts            # API test stubs
└── journey.test.ts        # Journey/E2E test stubs

docs.title: Python Output
docs.text:
generated-tests/
├── conftest.py            # pytest fixtures
├── test_functional.py     # Functional test stubs
├── test_api.py            # API test stubs
└── test_journey.py        # Journey/E2E test stubs

docs.title: Traceability
docs.text:
Generated tests include source references for traceability:

func TestTC001_UserLogin(t *testing.T) {
    // Source: REQ-AUTH-001
    // TPD: Section 3, Row 1
    t.Skip("TODO: Implement test")
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• synthesize - Generate TPD from PRD/TRD
  • eval - Evaluate specifications

docs.title: graph
docs.text: Manage requirement graphs using Graphize integration.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The graph command extracts requirement graphs from spec files, enabling traceability analysis and visualization. It integrates with graphize for graph operations.

docs.title: extract
docs.text:
Extract a requirement graph from all specs in the project.

visionspec graph extract

Output:

  • Creates .graphize/spec-graph.json in the project directory
  • Prints node count summary by type

Example:

Extracted graph with 45 nodes and 32 edges
Saved to: docs/specs/user-onboarding/.graphize/spec-graph.json

Node types:
  spec: 4
  section: 18
  requirement: 12
  user_story: 6
  constraint: 3
  decision: 2

docs.title: export
docs.text:
Export the graph to various formats for visualization or analysis.

visionspec graph export [--format FORMAT] [--output DIR]

Flags:

Flag Default Description --format html Export format: html, graphml, json --output .graphize Output directory

Formats:

Format Output File Description html graph.html Interactive HTML visualization graphml spec-graph.graphml GraphML for graph tools (yEd, Gephi) json spec-graph.json Raw graph data

Examples:

# Export as interactive HTML
visionspec graph export --format html

# Export as GraphML for yEd
visionspec graph export --format graphml

# Export to custom directory
visionspec graph export --format html --output ./reports

docs.title: query
docs.text:
Query nodes in the graph by type or spec.

visionspec graph query [--type TYPE] [--spec SPEC]

Flags:

Flag Description --type Filter by node type (requirement, user_story, constraint, decision) --spec Filter by spec type (mrd, prd, uxd, trd)

Examples:

# List all requirements
visionspec graph query --type requirement

# List all PRD nodes
visionspec graph query --spec prd

# List constraints from TRD
visionspec graph query --type constraint --spec trd

Output:

Found 12 nodes

[requirement] The system shall support SSO login
  ID: prd_req_req_prd_1
  Spec: prd
  Text: The system shall support SSO login via SAML 2.0...

[requirement] Users must be able to reset passwords
  ID: prd_req_req_prd_2
  Spec: prd
  Text: Users must be able to reset passwords via email...

docs.title: Node Types
docs.text:
The graph extractor identifies the following node types from specs:

Type Source Pattern requirement PRD Statements with "shall", "must", "should" user_story PRD "As a... I want... so that..." acceptance_criteria PRD "Given/When/Then" patterns constraint MRD, TRD Sections titled "Constraints", "Limitations" decision TRD Sections titled "Decisions", "Architecture" section All Markdown headings spec All Root node for each spec file

docs.title: Edge Types
docs.text:
Relationships extracted between nodes:

Type Description contains Section contains requirements/decisions traces_to Requirement traces to technical decision derived_from TRD derived from PRD and MRD

docs.title: Graph Storage
docs.text:
Graphs are stored in the .graphize/ directory within each project:

docs/specs/{project}/
└── .graphize/
    ├── spec-graph.json      # Primary graph data
    ├── spec-graph.graphml   # GraphML export (if generated)
    └── graph.html           # HTML visualization (if generated)

docs.title: Integration with Graphize
docs.text:
The graph command uses:

  • graphize v0.3.0 - Graph extraction and export
  • graphfs v0.2.0 - Graph data structures

For advanced graph analysis, you can use the graphize CLI directly on the exported JSON:

# Install graphize
go install github.com/plexusone/graphize/cmd/graphize@latest

# Analyze the spec graph
graphize analyze .graphize/spec-graph.json

# Generate detailed report
graphize report .graphize/spec-graph.json

docs.title: hooks
docs.text: Manage Git hooks for automatic spec validation.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: visionspec hooks <subcommand> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The hooks command manages Git hooks that automatically validate specifications during git operations. This ensures spec quality is maintained throughout the development workflow.

docs.title: Subcommands
docs.text: Subcommand Description install Install visionspec Git hooks uninstall Remove visionspec Git hooks status Show installed hooks status

docs.title: Supported Hooks
docs.text: Hook Trigger Action pre-commit Before commit Lint changed spec files pre-push Before push Evaluate specs, check for blockers

docs.title: Install Hooks
docs.text:
# Install all hooks
visionspec hooks install

# Install specific hooks
visionspec hooks install --hooks pre-commit,pre-push

docs.title: Uninstall Hooks
docs.text:
# Remove all visionspec hooks
visionspec hooks uninstall

# Remove specific hooks
visionspec hooks uninstall --hooks pre-commit

docs.title: Check Status
docs.text:
# Show status of all hooks
visionspec hooks status

docs.title: pre-commit
docs.text:
Runs before each commit to validate changed spec files:

# Automatically runs on git commit
git commit -m "Update PRD"

# Hook output:
# [visionspec] Linting changed specs...
# [visionspec] ✓ docs/specs/myproject/source/prd.md
# [visionspec] All specs valid

If validation fails:

# [visionspec] Linting changed specs...
# [visionspec] ✗ docs/specs/myproject/source/prd.md
#   - Missing required section: User Stories
# [visionspec] Commit blocked: fix spec errors first

docs.title: pre-push
docs.text:
Runs before push to evaluate specs and check for blockers:

# Automatically runs on git push
git push origin main

# Hook output:
# [visionspec] Evaluating specs before push...
# [visionspec] ✓ PRD score: 8.2 (passing)
# [visionspec] ✓ TRD score: 7.8 (passing)
# [visionspec] Push allowed

If evaluation fails:

# [visionspec] Evaluating specs before push...
# [visionspec] ✗ PRD score: 5.5 (failing)
#   - Critical: Missing acceptance criteria
# [visionspec] Push blocked: resolve critical findings

docs.title: Status Output
docs.text:
$ visionspec hooks status

Git Hooks Status
────────────────────────────────────
Hook          Installed  VisionSpec
────────────────────────────────────
pre-commit    ✓          ✓
pre-push      ✓          ✓
commit-msg    ✗          -
post-commit   ✗          -
────────────────────────────────────

Hooks directory: /path/to/repo/.git/hooks

docs.title: Backup and Restore
docs.text:
When installing hooks, existing hooks are backed up:

$ visionspec hooks install

Installing hooks...
  pre-commit: backed up existing → pre-commit.backup
  pre-commit: installed
  pre-push: installed

Hooks installed successfully.

When uninstalling, backups are restored:

$ visionspec hooks uninstall

Uninstalling hooks...
  pre-commit: removed
  pre-commit: restored from backup
  pre-push: removed

Hooks uninstalled successfully.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Hooks can be configured in visionspec.yaml:

hooks:
  enabled: true
  pre_commit:
    enabled: true
    lint_only: true  # Only lint, don't evaluate
  pre_push:
    enabled: true
    require_passing: true  # Block push if evaluation fails
    min_score: 7.0  # Minimum score to pass

docs.title: CI Integration
docs.text:
For CI environments where hooks should be skipped:

# Skip hooks with git flag
git commit --no-verify -m "CI commit"
git push --no-verify

# Or set environment variable
VISIONSPEC_SKIP_HOOKS=1 git commit -m "CI commit"

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• lint - Validate spec structure
  • eval - Evaluate spec quality
  • status - Check project status

docs.title: visionspec init
docs.text: Initialize a new VisionSpec project.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: visionspec init <project-name>

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Creates a new project with the standard VisionSpec directory structure:

docs/specs/{project}/
├── source/
│   ├── mrd.md
│   ├── prd.md
│   └── uxd.md
├── gtm/
├── technical/
├── eval/
└── visionspec.yaml

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Description project-name Yes Name of the project (kebab-case)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Create a new project
visionspec init user-onboarding

# Create another project
visionspec init payment-integration

docs.title: visionspec.yaml
docs.text:
Project configuration file:

name: user-onboarding
description: ""
version: 0.1.0

docs.title: Source Spec Placeholders
docs.text:
The source/ directory contains placeholder markdown files:

  • mrd.md - Market Requirements Document
  • prd.md - Product Requirements Document
  • uxd.md - User Experience Design

docs.title: Project Naming
docs.text:
Project names should be:

  • Lowercase
  • Hyphen-separated (kebab-case)
  • Descriptive

Good: user-onboarding, payment-flow, admin-dashboard

Bad: UserOnboarding, payment_flow, ADMIN

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• lint - Validate project structure
  • status - Check project readiness

docs.title: visionspec lint
docs.text: Validate project directory structure and naming conventions.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: visionspec lint [project] [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Validates that the project follows VisionSpec conventions:

  • Directory structure matches canonical layout
  • File naming follows conventions (lowercase specs, kebab-case projects)
  • Required directories exist
  • Config file is valid

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Description project No Project name to lint (lints all if omitted)

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Default Description --format text Output format: text or json --ci false Exit with non-zero code if lint fails

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Lint all projects
visionspec lint

# Lint specific project
visionspec lint user-onboarding

# JSON output for CI
visionspec lint --format json

# CI mode (exit 1 on failure)
visionspec lint --ci

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
The linter checks for the expected directory layout:

docs/specs/{project}/
├── source/       # Required
├── gtm/          # Required
├── technical/    # Required
├── eval/         # Required
└── visionspec.yaml  # Required

docs.title: Naming Conventions
docs.text: Element Convention Valid Invalid Project directory kebab-case user-onboarding UserOnboarding Spec files lowercase.md mrd.md MRD.md Eval files lowercase.eval.json mrd.eval.json MRD_eval.json

docs.title: Config File
docs.text:
The visionspec.yaml file must exist and be valid YAML with required fields:

name: project-name      # Required
description: "..."      # Optional
version: 0.1.0          # Optional

docs.title: Text Output
docs.text:
Linting project: user-onboarding
  [+] Directory structure valid
  [+] Config file valid
  [+] Naming conventions followed

Result: PASSED

docs.title: JSON Output
docs.text:
{
  "project": "user-onboarding",
  "passed": true,
  "errors": [],
  "warnings": []
}

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Description 0 All checks passed 1 Lint failures (with --ci flag)

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• init - Create a new project
  • status - Check project readiness

docs.title: metrics
docs.text: View evaluation and reconciliation metrics dashboard.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: visionspec metrics [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The metrics command displays a dashboard of evaluation and reconciliation metrics for a project. It aggregates scores, findings, and trends across all specification types.

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Description -p, --project Project name --format Output format: text, json, html (default: text) --since Show metrics since date (YYYY-MM-DD) --compare Compare with previous evaluation

docs.title: Basic Metrics
docs.text:
# Show metrics for project
visionspec metrics -p myproject

docs.title: Output Formats
docs.text:
# Text output (default)
visionspec metrics -p myproject

# JSON output for programmatic use
visionspec metrics -p myproject --format json

# HTML output for reports
visionspec metrics -p myproject --format html > metrics.html

docs.title: Historical Comparison
docs.text:
# Show metrics since specific date
visionspec metrics -p myproject --since 2024-01-01

# Compare with previous evaluation
visionspec metrics -p myproject --compare

docs.title: Text Format
docs.text:
╭─────────────────────────────────────────╮
│         Evaluation Metrics              │
│         user-onboarding                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Spec       Score    Findings   Status   │
│ ──────────────────────────────────────  │
│ MRD        8.5      2 low      ✓ Pass   │
│ PRD        7.8      1 medium   ✓ Pass   │
│ UXD        7.2      3 medium   ✓ Pass   │
│ TRD        8.1      1 low      ✓ Pass   │
│ IRD        7.5      2 low      ✓ Pass   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Overall    7.8      9 total    ✓ Ready  │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────╯

Reconciliation Status:
  Last reconciled: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
  Conflicts resolved: 3
  Manual interventions: 1

docs.title: Metrics Report
docs.text:
Example JSON output:

{
  "project": "user-onboarding",
  "generated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "evaluations": {
    "mrd": {
      "score": 8.5,
      "findings": {"low": 2, "medium": 0, "high": 0, "critical": 0},
      "passing": true
    },
    "prd": {
      "score": 7.8,
      "findings": {"low": 0, "medium": 1, "high": 0, "critical": 0},
      "passing": true
    }
  },
  "reconciliation": {
    "last_run": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    "conflicts_resolved": 3,
    "manual_interventions": 1
  },
  "summary": {
    "average_score": 7.8,
    "total_findings": 9,
    "specs_passing": 5,
    "specs_failing": 0,
